title: marrubium candidissimum subsp. cylleneum
intro: Marrubium candidissimum subsp. cylleneum, also known as Horehound, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Horehound include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as soothing digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome and indigestion. Horehound is typically prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its therapeutic properties.
